sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
Right from the start/finish straight, brake hard into Turn 1, a tight left-hander, at around the 100m board and row down the gears to 2nd. Turn 2 quickly follows: a tight right, where traction is key. Then, Turn 3 is a 90-degree left. This sequence demands precision — its easy to overheat the rears and spin here if you push too hard too early in a racing situation.

You approach a right-left-right flick at medium speed. These corners require smooth throttle input and clean positioning. Stay in 3rd or 4th gear, depending on your setup, and avoid riding the curbs aggressively — the walls are close and visibility is limited at night.

This corner opens up into a sweeping exit — keep your steering light and prepare for the straight that follows. Focus on a tidy line and use all the track on exit to carry momentum.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
Brake hard before the entry to this tricky left-right chicane that leads onto Las Vegas Boulevard. Drop to 2nd gear, and stay off the sausage kerbs, they can chuck your car into the wall in an instant. A strong exit is vital because this leads onto the longest full-throttle section of the track.

This massive straight is flat out, with minor kinks that are taken easily at full throttle. It includes one of the DRS zones, essential for overtaking on street tracks like Las Vegas. Use this to line up a move into the heavy braking zone at Turn 12.

At the end of the Strip, brake from over 330 km/h down to 2nd gear for a sharp 90-degree left. This is a prime overtaking spot. Watch for lock-ups and understeer — low-grip tarmac here makes braking tricky, especially late in the race when your tyres are fading away.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
This is a flowing change of direction taken in 3rd gear. Keep the car stable and avoid excessive kerb usage. The walls narrow here, so focus on hitting the apexes without being aggressive on kerbs.

A tight right-left combo that leads into the final corner. Braking stability and traction are key, especially late in the race when tyre wear sets in. Be patient on the throttle and keep the car balanced.

The final corner is a slightly open left that slingshots you back onto the start/finish straight. Take it in 3rd gear, with a clean exit being absolutely essential to maintain top speed. Open DRS as early as possible and complete the lap on the right-hand side of the circuit, ready for turn one again.
